data: {"content_index":0,"item_id":"","logprobs":[],"output_index":1,"sequence_number":6221,"text":"\u003canalysis\u003e\n[Chronological Review: Conversation started with the user asking to redesign a page to be “现代化和紧凑一点”, especially questioning whether “姓名部门 在线情况是不是可以挪到小火苗那，开始对话的按钮还有没有必要”. Initial inspection found the currently open frontend `frontend/src/App.tsx` was a large-screen dashboard and not the mobile-like page in the screenshot. A repository text search then located the actual target UI in `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts` (and mirrored dev template in `dashboard.dev.ts`) with strings like “开始新会话”, “基础设置”, “监控代理”, “Powered by Zhi.Chen”. The agent refactored the extension webview template: moved identity info into the header area, removed the separate user card, downgraded/then later removed the “新会话” button, tightened spacing, added responsive behavior, and made offline status visually stronger.  \nNext, the user asked to “处理问题并更新扩展”. Investigation showed TypeScript compile errors across the extension due to missing runtime type declarations (`setInterval`, `console`, `process`, `Buffer`, `fetch`, etc.). The fix was to update `vscode-extension/tsconfig.json` to include Node/VS Code/Jest types, after which compile errors disappeared. The extension was then compiled and packaged into VSIX, and installed manually into the VS Code extensions directory because direct CLI install behavior was inconsistent. Version was bumped from `0.1.0` to `0.1.1`, then later to `0.1.2` as UI redesign continued.  \nThe user then asked for further UI improvement: remove the small flame icon and make the layout truly comfortable. The header and panel layout were redesigned again in `vscode-extension/src/dashboard.ts`: flame icon removed, vertical rail removed, top section turned into a calmer text-first information layout, top toolbar consolidated address/proxy/refresh, cards and setting sections restyled, and some dead webview code cleaned up. The extension was rebuilt and installed as `0.1.2`.  \nThe user then pointed out that the identity card still had too much blank space and requested “横向的展示一些”. The agent identified the cause: the header grid stretched the identity card vertically. It changed the header alignment and reworked the identity card to show department and employee ID as horizontal pills, reducing whitespace and syncing the compiled output into the installed `0.1.2` extension.  \nFinally, the user shifted to packaging/build concerns: “检查一下当前脚本有没有问题，生成各个系统扩展包的脚本”, then confirmed “需要” when asked implicitly whether to continue. Analysis showed the previous packaging flow claimed cross-platform VSIX support but actually only had a Windows client binary and a Windows-only client build script. The extension packaging scripts were fixed to validate target-specific binaries, and then the work expanded to build a real cross-platform client build chain. The last phase focused on adding non-Windows client implementations, adding `client/build.ps1`, wiring `vscode-extension/build.ps1` to auto-build missing client binaries, and validating packaging for win/mac/mac-arm/linux. This last validation phase is what directly triggered summarization.]\n\n[Intent Mapping: \n1.
